focus: Cohort replay (51-file, singleton) + 0.26.8 swap probe + Hypothesis C surface scan
stopCondition: continue (B2 mechanism narrowed to runtime-context-or-mis-attribution; iter 5 should run long-loop stress + diagnostics-write audit)

title: Cohort replay: 41 OK / 9 B1 / 0 B2
summary: Replaying full 51-file OOB cohort through one shared parserInstance (web-tree-sitter@0.24.7) produces 41 successful parses, 9 B1 throws (all .sh), and ZERO memory-access-out-of-bounds. Cumulative-history-in-process REJECTED at 51-file horizon.

title: web-tree-sitter@0.26.8 swap rejects vendored wasms with 'need dylink section'
summary: 0.26.8 hard-fails Language.load on all 4 vendored tree-sitter-wasms@0.1.13 grammars at failIf(name2 !== 'dylink.0'). Vendored wasms contain a dylink string but lack the 'dylink.0' section name format 0.26.8 requires. Version-bump remediation is NOT a single-line fix — needs co-bump + grammar rebuild.

title: B1 is content-conditional within bash grammar — 9 of 33 cohort .sh files throw
summary: Cohort replay shows 9 of 33 .sh files throw B1; the remaining 24 .sh files parse cleanly through the same singleton with the same tree-sitter-bash.wasm. The proxy stub at tree-sitter.js:1163-1180 fires only when the parsed source actually invokes external_scanner_reset, not on every bash parse. Strategy §11 Q5 framing 'every bash file fails' is incorrect.

title: Hypothesis C surface scan: '.ts'-suffix imports correlate 6/10 OOB vs 0/5 clean
summary: 6 of 10 OOB .ts files use 'import ... from "./foo.ts"' (explicit .ts extension); 0 of 5 size-matched clean samples carry the pattern. Suggestive but not deterministic — 4 OOB victims lack the pattern. Combined with F-4.5 showing all 10 .ts OOB files parse cleanly anyway, content-syntax is downgraded to 'possible secondary stressor, unproven'.

title: All 10 OOB .ts files parse cleanly under singleton replay
summary: Indices [2-11] of cohort replay all returned OK — vitest.phase-k.config.ts (20L), runners/common.ts, phase-k-v1-0-4-stress.test.ts (670L), and all 5 hook-runner files. Combined with F-3.4 single-file isolation, this is the second independent layer of evidence that .ts OOB rows in parse_diagnostics do NOT reproduce as parser-level OOBs in a clean Node process. Strong support for parse_diagnostics mis-attribution hypothesis.

title: parse_diagnostics lacks insertion-order column — replay used last_seen_at proxy
summary: Schema is (file_path PRIMARY KEY, error_message, error_count, last_seen_at) — no rowid-equivalent. Replay used ORDER BY last_seen_at, file_path as closest proxy to original scan order. Methodology caveat for cumulative-history reasoning. Plan-input note: Phase 2 schema migration should add scan_seq INTEGER autoincrement to parse_diagnostics for reproducible ordering.
